'''Shigetaka''', [[Masuyama Masakage]], [[#Genealogy|others]] | spouse = Main wife: '''Toyo''' (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]; 瑞泰院){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Concubines: Rio (daughter of retainer Iida Sonchoku),
Ruso (sister of Muto Gunji, retainer of the Satake clan),
Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the 21st head of the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]] and served as the 8th lord of the [[:en:Chōfu Domain|Chōfu Domain]] and later the 7th lord of the [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. His [[:en:Given name|Given name]] evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]] as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]]. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in [[:en:Hagi|Hagi]]. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the 21st head of the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]] and served as the 8th lord of the [[:en:Chōfu Domain|Chōfu Domain]] and later the 7th lord of the [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. His [[:en:Given name|Given name]] evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]] as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]]. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in [[:en:Hagi|Hagi]]. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Adoptive father: ''[[Mōri Munemichi]]'' | siblings = Enjō-in (wife of [[Arima Kazukuni]]), [[Mōri Motoyuki|Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane|Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu|Hironobu]],
'''Shigetaka''', [[Masuyama Masakage]], [[#Genealogy|others]] | spouse = Main wife: '''Toyo''' (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]; 瑞泰院){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Concubines: Rio (daughter of retainer Iida Sonchoku),
Ruso (sister of Muto Gunji, retainer of the Satake clan),
Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the 21st head of the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]] and served as the 8th lord of the [[:en:Chōfu Domain|Chōfu Domain]] and later the 7th lord of the [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. His [[:en:Given name|Given name]] evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]] as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]]. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in [[:en:Hagi|Hagi]]. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|WD=Q413694|lt=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] [[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] [[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Mouri Shigenari.jpg|birth_date=1725; {{birth date|1725|10|15}}|death_date=1789; {{death date|1789|11|23}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|daimyo|WD=Q61982}}|spouse={{ill|Tachibana Sadayoshi|WD=Q11598206}}|children={{ill|Mōri Chikaaki|WD=Q11547689}}; {{ill|Mōri Haruchika|WD=Q11547668}}; {{ill|Mōri Masamitsu|WD=Q11547575}}; {{ill|Mōri Masayoshi|WD=Q11547577}}; {{ill|Q109669400|WD=Q109669400}}; {{ill|Q109669437|WD=Q109669437}}; {{ill|Q109669491|WD=Q109669491}}; {{ill|Q109669534|WD=Q109669534}}|father={{ill|Mōri Masahiro|WD=Q11547574}}|mother={{ill|Q109671273|WD=Q109671273}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11547699]] Wikidata name: Mōri Shigetaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Samurai | name = Mōri Shigetaka | image = Mouri Shigenari.jpg | image_size = 200px | caption = Portrait of Mōri Shigetaka | period = Mid to Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 10, [[9th month|September 10]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} (October 15, [[1725]]) | death_date = {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 1, [[10th month|October 7]] (November 23, [[1789]]){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | birth_name = Iwanojō (infant name){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Later names: {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=諱}}: Motofusa{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Masatakaまさたか, Shigenari → Shigetaka | posthumous_name = Eiun Kō{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | dharma_name = Eiuninden Mae Nishu Taishu Shihin Riyobu Taikō Ulin Jōkei Daikōshi{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | resting_place = [[Tōkō-ji (Hagi)|Tōkō-ji]], [[Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | court_Rank = Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) Governor of Kai Province, Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (従四位下) Chamberlain (侍従) and Senior Assistant Minister of the Imperial Household (大膳大夫), Lesser Captain of the Left Imperial Guard (左近衛少将), Minister of Ceremonial (式部大輔), posthumously Senior Third Rank (従三位) in [[Meiji 41]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate]] | lord = | domain = {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain}} → {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}} | clan = {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}} | parents = Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Mother: Shōzen-in (née Iida){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Adoptive father: ''[[Mōri Munemichi]]'' | siblings = Enjō-in (wife of [[Arima Kazukuni]]), [[Mōri Motoyuki|Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane|Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu|Hironobu]],
'''Shigetaka''', [[Masuyama Masakage]], [[#Genealogy|others]] | spouse = Main wife: '''Toyo''' (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]; 瑞泰院){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Concubines: Rio (daughter of retainer Iida Sonchoku),
Ruso (sister of Muto Gunji, retainer of the Satake clan),
Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. He was the 21st head of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}} and served as the 8th lord of the {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain}} and later the 7th lord of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}}. His {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=Given name}} evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}} as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, {{ill|Tokugawa Ienari|en|Tokugawa Ienari|WD=Q439717|lt=Tokugawa Ienari}}. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|WD=Q413694|lt=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. He was the 21st head of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}} and served as the 8th lord of the {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain}} and later the 7th lord of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}}. His {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=Given name}} evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}} as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, {{ill|Tokugawa Ienari|en|Tokugawa Ienari|WD=Q439717|lt=Tokugawa Ienari}}. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|WD=Q413694|lt=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] [[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] [[ja:毛利重就]][[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Mouri Shigenari.jpg|birth_date=1725; {{birth date|1725|10|15}}|death_date=1789; {{death date|1789|11|23}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|occupation={{ill|daimyo|WD=Q61982|1=Daimyo}}|spouse={{ill|Tachibana Sadayoshi|WD=Q11598206|1=Tachibana Sadayoshi}}|children={{ill|Mōri Chikaaki|WD=Q11547689|1=Mōri Chikaaki}}; {{ill|Mōri Haruchika|WD=Q11547668|1=Mōri Haruchika}}; {{ill|Mōri Masamitsu|WD=Q11547575|1=Mōri Masamitsu}}; {{ill|Mōri Masayoshi|WD=Q11547577|1=Mōri Masayoshi}}; {{ill|Q109669400|WD=Q109669400|1=Q109669400}}; {{ill|Q109669437|WD=Q109669437|1=Q109669437}}; {{ill|Q109669491|WD=Q109669491|1=Q109669491}}; {{ill|Q109669534|WD=Q109669534|1=Q109669534}}|father={{ill|Mōri Masahiro|WD=Q11547574|1=Mōri Masahiro}}|mother={{ill|Q109671273|WD=Q109671273|1=Q109671273}}}} {{Infobox Samurai | name = Mōri Shigetaka | image = Mouri Shigenari.jpg | image_size = 200px | caption = Portrait of Mōri Shigetaka | period = Mid to Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 10, [[9th month|September 10]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} (October 15, [[1725]]) | death_date = {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 1, [[10th month|October 7]] (November 23, [[1789]]){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | birth_name = Iwanojō (infant name){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Later names: {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=諱|1=Given name}}: Motofusa{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Masatakaまさたか, Shigenari → Shigetaka | posthumous_name = Eiun Kō{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | dharma_name = Eiuninden Mae Nishu Taishu Shihin Riyobu Taikō Ulin Jōkei Daikōshi{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | resting_place = [[Tōkō-ji (Hagi)|Tōkō-ji]], [[Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | court_Rank = Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) Governor of Kai Province, Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (従四位下) Chamberlain (侍従) and Senior Assistant Minister of the Imperial Household (大膳大夫), Lesser Captain of the Left Imperial Guard (左近衛少将), Minister of Ceremonial (式部大輔), posthumously Senior Third Rank (従三位) in [[Meiji 41]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate]] | lord = | domain = {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain|1=Chōfu Domain}} → {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}} | clan = {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} | parents = Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Mother: Shōzen-in (née Iida){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Adoptive father: ''[[Mōri Munemichi]]'' | siblings = Enjō-in (wife of [[Arima Kazukuni]]), [[Mōri Motoyuki|Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane|Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu|Hironobu]],
'''Shigetaka''', [[Masuyama Masakage]], [[#Genealogy|others]] | spouse = Main wife: '''Toyo''' (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]; 瑞泰院){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Concubines: Rio (daughter of retainer Iida Sonchoku),
Ruso (sister of Muto Gunji, retainer of the Satake clan),
Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was the 21st head of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} and served as the 8th lord of the {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain|1=Chōfu Domain}} and later the 7th lord of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}}. His {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=Given name|1=Given name}} evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}} as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, {{ill|Tokugawa Ienari|en|Tokugawa Ienari|WD=Q439717|lt=Tokugawa Ienari|1=Tokugawa Ienari}}. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|WD=Q413694|lt=Hagi|1=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

Later names: {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=諱|1=Given name}}: Motofusa{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Masatakaまさたか, Shigenari → Shigetaka | posthumous_name = Eiun Kō{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | dharma_name = Eiuninden Mae Nishu Taishu Shihin Riyobu Taikō Ulin Jōkei Daikōshi{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | resting_place = [[Tōkō-ji (Hagi)|Tōkō-ji]], [[Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | court_Rank = Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) Governor of Kai Province, Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (従四位下) Chamberlain (侍従) and Senior Assistant Minister of the Imperial Household (大膳大夫), Lesser Captain of the Left Imperial Guard (左近衛少将), Minister of Ceremonial (式部大輔), posthumously Senior Third Rank (従三位) in [[Meiji 41]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}} | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate]] | lord = | domain = {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain|1=Chōfu Domain}} → {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}} | clan = {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} | parents = Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}, Mother: Shōzen-in (née Iida){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Adoptive father: ''[[Mōri Munemichi]]'' | siblings = Enjō-in (wife of [[Arima Kazukuni]]), [[Mōri Motoyuki|Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane|Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu|Hironobu]],
'''Shigetaka''', [[Masuyama Masakage]], [[#Genealogy|others]] | spouse = Main wife: '''Toyo''' (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]; 瑞泰院){{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=102}}
Concubines: Rio (daughter of retainer Iida Sonchoku),
Ruso (sister of Muto Gunji, retainer of the Satake clan),
Taneori (daughter of retainer Kōno Michisada) | children = '''[[Mōri Masamitsu|Masamitsu]]''', '''[[Mōri Haruchika|Haruchika]]''', [[Mōri Masayoshi|Masayoshi]], [[Mōri Chikayuki|Chikayuki]] (sixth son), Tomo (wife of [[Yamauchi Toyoyasu]]), Seiyo (wife of [[Arima Yoritaka]]), Kaho (wife of [[Matsudaira Nobutaka (Fujii)|Matsudaira Nobutaka]]), Sayo (wife of [[Takatsukasa Sukehira]]), Sumi (wife of [[Matsudaira Tadaaki (Okudaira)|Matsudaira Tadaaki]]), Nagako (wife of [[Mōri Masakuni]], later wife of [[Maeda Toshinobu]]; Kenshō-in), a daughter (wife of [[Naito Nobuaki]]), Retsu (fiancée of [[Ii Naotomi]]), Tatsuko (fiancée of [[Konoe Tsuneyuki]])
Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was the 21st head of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} and served as the 8th lord of the {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|WD=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain|1=Chōfu Domain}} and later the 7th lord of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}}. His {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|WD=Q202444|lt=Given name|1=Given name}} evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}} as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, {{ill|Tokugawa Ienari|en|Tokugawa Ienari|WD=Q439717|lt=Tokugawa Ienari|1=Tokugawa Ienari}}. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|WD=Q413694|lt=Hagi|1=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:毛利重就]] [[ko:모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)]] {{wikidata link|Q11547699}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Adopted children: ''[[Mōri Shigehiro]]''{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, adopted daughter: ''Makoto'' (second wife of [[Matsudaira Yoshinaga]]{{Sfn|Tokiyama Yaha ed.|1916|p=103}}, [[#Genealogy|others]] | notable_works = }} '''Mōri Shigetaka''' (毛利 重就, ''Mōri Shigetaka'') was a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of the mid to late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was the 21st head of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} and served as the 8th lord of the {{ill|Chōfu Domain|en|Chōfu Domain|qid=Q903430|lt=Chōfu Domain|1=Chōfu Domain}} and later the 7th lord of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|qid=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}}. His {{ill|Given name|en|Given name|qid=Q202444|lt=Given name|1=Given name}} evolved from '''Motofusa''' (元房) to '''Masataka''' (匡敬), and finally to '''Shigenari''' (重就), before being further altered to Shigetaka. == Biography == Mōri Shigetaka was born on {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 10 (1725) as the 10th son of [[Mōri Masahiro]], lord of the Chōfu Domain. His [[childhood name]] was Iwanojō. Following a series of family deaths and circumstances, he succeeded as the lord of the Chōfu Domain. Later, in {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 1 (1751), he became the lord of the main {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|qid=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain}} as an [[adoptive heir]] due to the lack of an immediate successor. As a domain lord, Shigetaka implemented significant reforms to address fiscal difficulties caused by natural disasters and poor trade. He introduced new administrative practices, increased domain revenues through reclamation projects, and restructured the economy. Despite successes, his taxation policies also led to peasant uprisings. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1 (1781), Shigetaka altered the reading of his name from "Shigenari" to "Shigetaka" to avoid using the same character ("就") as the shōgun's heir, {{ill|Tokugawa Ienari|en|Tokugawa Ienari|qid=Q439717|lt=Tokugawa Ienari|1=Tokugawa Ienari}}. He retired the following year, passing the domain leadership to his fourth son, [[Mōri Haruchika]]. Shigetaka spent his later years in [[Mitajiri]] and died in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 1 (1789) at the age of 64. His grave is located at [[Tōkō-ji]] in {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|qid=Q413694|lt=Hagi|1=Hagi}}. == Genealogy == See "Hagi City History, Volume 1" for further details. * Father: [[Mōri Masahiro]] (1675–1729) * Mother: Shōzen-in, daughter of the Iida clan * Siblings: [[Mōri Motoyuki]], [[Mōri Masatane]], [[Mōri Hironobu]], etc. * Spouse: Toyo (daughter of [[Tachibana Sadafuku]]) * Children: [[Mōri Masamitsu]], [[Mōri Haruchika]], etc. == Related Works == * Ogawa Kuniji, ''Mōri Shigetaka'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2003) ISBN 4-642-05226-7 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.hofu.yamaguchi.jp/webhis/6kinsei/06-12/6-12.html Hofu and Mōri Shigetaka] - Hofu Web History Museum * [http://www.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/gyosei/kanko/brand/bakumatsu/index.html Mōri Shigetaka and the Foundation of Hagi Domain Reforms] - Yamaguchi Brand Story {{Chōfu Domain Lords}} {{Chōshū Domain Lords}} {{translated page|ja|毛利重就|version=104495066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11547699|ja|毛利重就|ko|모리 시게타카 (에도 시대)}} [[Category:1725 births]] [[Category:1789 deaths]] [[Category:Chōfu Mōri Family]] [[Category:Deified Daimyō of the Edo Period]] [[Category:Hagi Mōri Family]] [[Category:Lords of Chōfu Domain]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū]] [[Category:Lords of Chōshū|Shigetaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
